channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Gilmar virou cabo eleitoral de senadores anti-STF em 2026
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Gilmar Mendes used his authority to annul decisions from the Senate's CPI investigating organized crime, acting to protect his ally Dias Toffoli. This positions him as an electoral operative for senators opposing the STF in 2026.

item.title: Bola de cristal
item.description: Brazil's national team under Carlo Ancelotti will announce its 2026 World Cup squad on May 18. Until then, speculation surrounds the 26 selected players, including Neymar's uncertain inclusion. Having called up 55 players since May 2025, Ancelotti shows a mix of certainties — such as Marquinhos and Gabriel Magalhães as starting center-backs — and doubts in other positions.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro diz que condução da economia em um governo seu será parecida com a do pai
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro states that his economic management, if elected, would resemble that of his father Jair Bolsonaro's government, citing post-pandemic growth above China's and inflation below the U.S. He proposes spending less than revenue, cutting taxes, and refrains from naming economic team members, including Paulo Guedes. Despite dismissing polls, he acknowledges a growing trend in voter intention and criticizes the PT and Lula as 'expired merchandise'.

item.title: Prefeito de Maceió não irá a lançamento de pré-candidatura de Lira ao Senado e evidencia distanciamento
item.description: Maceió's mayor, JHC (PL), announced he will not attend the launch of former House Speaker Arthur Lira's (PP) Senate pre-candidacy, scheduled for Friday in the Alagoas capital. His absence signals a growing political distance between the two leaders, who previously maintained alliances. This move may indicate a reconfiguration of political forces in Alagoas ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Lula pressiona governadores a zerar ICMS do diesel para conter impacto da guerra
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva urged governors to adopt a proposal to zero the ICMS tax on diesel imports, offering to compensate half of the states' lost revenue. The measure aims to counter the impact of rising international oil prices on domestic fuel costs. It is part of a federal government effort to mitigate the economic effects of the war in Ukraine on Brazil.

item.title: Deputada que fez blackface e se diz branca recebeu R$ 1.593 de fundo para negros
item.description: State Deputy Fabiana Bolsonaro (PL-SP) received R$1,593.33 from the Special Campaign Financing Fund in the 2022 elections after self-declaring as parda (brown), despite previously identifying as white and facing accusations of wearing blackface. The funds, partially intended for Black candidacies, were identified by fellow deputy Monica Seixas (PSOL-SP).

item.title: O revés do PL no TSE em ação contra homenagem a Lula no Carnaval do Rio
item.description: The electoral corregedor of the TSE, Antonio Carlos Ferreira, rejected a PL request for anticipatory evidence production in a case against President Lula over the Acadêmicos de Niterói samba parade honoring the petista during Rio's Carnival. Ferreira argued the party seeks only to compile publicly available data without demonstrating a need for judicial intervention, characterizing the move as an improper exploratory use of judicial proceedings.

item.title: Erika Hilton pede investigação sobre ministros do STF no caso Master
item.description: Federal Deputy Erika Hilton (Psol-SP) called for an investigation into potential conflicts of interest involving Supreme Court Ministers Dias Toffoli and Alexandre de Moraes in the Banco Master case. She highlighted the hiring of Moraes’ wife to defend the bank for R$ 129 million in fees, Toffoli’s familial business ties to funds linked to the bank, and unverified reports of messages between banker Daniel Vorcaro and Moraes. Hilton stressed the need for transparency and urged Toffoli to recuse himself from any deliberations on the case to preserve the Court’s impartiality.

item.title: Padre Kelmon anuncia pré-candidatura a deputado
item.description: Priest Kelmon Luis Souza announced his pre-candidacy for federal deputy from São Paulo under the PL party on March 18, 2026, stating the decision fulfills a 'mission from God' and the 'call of our leader'. He made the announcement via a post on X (formerly Twitter), accompanied by a photo with former President Jair Bolsonaro, writing: 'Together we will rescue Brazil'.

item.title: Rafael Greca deixa partido de Ratinho Junior e se filia ao MDB mirando governo do PR
item.description: Former Curitiba mayor Rafael Greca announced he is leaving the PSD and joining the MDB to pursue the governorship of Paraná in 2026. His departure follows a perceived lack of space in the PSD, as Governor Ratinho Junior has signaled preference for Guto Silva as the party's candidate. Greca's move reflects a broader reconfiguration of political alliances in the state, also influenced by Sergio Moro's shift to the PL and endorsement of Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: Em evento em SP, Lula crítica Tarcísio e diz que ele não gosta de receber prefeitos: 'são pouco e mal-recebidos pelo governo do estado'
item.description: President Lula criticized Governor Tarcísio de Freitas for not receiving mayors in a republican manner at the Palácio dos Bandeirantes, stating they are 'few and poorly received'. Lula emphasized that his government attends to all mayors regardless of party, reinforcing federalism and the Ministry of Cities. The event took place during the 17th Federative Caravan in São Paulo, where Haddad confirmed his departure from the Ministry of Finance to run for governor against Tarcísio in 2026.

item.title: STF derruba voto aberto em eleição indireta para governador do Rio
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Luiz Fux struck down two provisions of Rio de Janeiro state law governing indirect elections for governor: open voting and the 24-hour desincompatibilization period. The ruling aims to ensure voting freedom and equality in indirect elections by applying the same guarantees as direct elections, citing risks of political violence and undue influence in the state legislature.

item.title: STF começa nesta sexta (20) o julgamento sobre privatização da Sabesp
item.description: The STF begins judging the actions challenging the privatization of Sabesp, with rapporteur Minister Cristiano Zanin. The actions, filed by the PT and a left-wing coalition, allege conflict of interest, lack of technical studies, and a single-bidder auction. Sabesp defends the process, stating that Karla Bertocco Trindade had no voting power and that non-disclosure of the minimum value prevented anchoring and encouraged competitive bids.

item.title: TSE rejeita pedido do PL para investigar Lula por desfile de escola de samba que o homenageou
item.description: The TSE rejected a PL request to investigate Lula over alleged use of public funds in the Acadêmicos de Niterói samba school parade honoring him during Carnival. Correitor-General Antonio Carlos Ferreira ruled the request sought indiscriminate information gathering without proving judicial intervention was indispensable, dismissing the case without evaluating its merits.

item.title: Deputada que fez ‘blackface’ na Alesp se declarou parda à Justiça Eleitoral; Erika Hilton pede investigação
item.description: State Deputy Fabiana Bolsonaro (PL) performed blackface in the São Paulo Legislative Assembly to criticize federal Deputy Erika Hilton (PSOL-SP), claiming she is white and has benefited from white privilege. Bolsonaro had self-declared as parda (brown/mixed-race) in her 2022 electoral registration, which Hilton alleges was fraudulent to access campaign funds from the Fundão, prompting a request for an electoral fraud investigation. Other lawmakers have also filed ethics complaints and police reports for potential racism.

item.title: Notícias - Partido Novo
item.description: The New Party invites interested individuals to affiliate by April 4 to run as candidates in 2026, emphasizing lower taxes, fighting censorship, supporting entrepreneurs, and respecting honest citizens. The party states it defends right-wing values and aims to increase participation from those aligned with its principles.

item.title: PL tenta, mas não consegue avançar na investigação do carnaval de Lula na Sapucaí
item.description: TSE Minister Antonio Carlos Ferreira rejected a PL lawsuit seeking information about the Acadêmicos de Niterói parade honoring Lula, stating the party failed to demonstrate prior attempts to obtain the data and that the request constituted an impermissible exploratory fishing expedition. The case was dismissed.

item.title: Quem é Dario Durigan, que vai assumir Ministério da Fazenda no lugar de Haddad
item.description: Dario Durigan, executive secretary of the Ministry of Finance, will assume the portfolio after Fernando Haddad's departure to run for governor of São Paulo. Durigan, experienced in revenue recomposition measures, articulation of the consumption tax reform, and state debt renegotiation, previously worked with Haddad in São Paulo City Hall and as Director of Public Policy at WhatsApp. He will face challenges coordinating economic policy during Lula's re-election campaign, with agenda items including review of social benefits and reform of payroll charges.

item.title: Lula diz que Tarcísio recebe mal prefeitos de São Paulo
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that São Paulo mayors are poorly received by Governor Tarcísio de Freitas, suggesting they should first seek solutions at the state level before going to Brasília. He emphasized that the federal government receives all mayors with dignity, regardless of ideology, during the 17th Federative Caravan at Expo Center Norte.

item.title: Lula diz que ficou triste com corte de apenas 0,25% nos juros
item.description: President Lula expressed disappointment with the Central Bank's modest 0.25 percentage point cut to the Selic rate, stating he had expected a reduction of at least 0.5%. He attributed the cautious move to the war in Iran, triggered by a U.S. attack on February 28, which he linked to rising global oil prices and economic instability. Lula criticized U.S. President Donald Trump for acting as if he 'owned the world' and urged the UN Security Council to intervene to prevent conflict, stressing that the world needs peace, education, and food — not war.

item.title: O movimento de Sergio Moro que pode esquentar ainda mais a disputa entre Lula e Flávio
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro is set to officially join the Liberal Party (PL), the party of former president Jair Bolsonaro, with the goal of running for governor of Paraná in 2026. The move, negotiated with Flávio Bolsonaro and Valdemar Costa Neto, aims to strengthen Bolsonaro’s base in the state, leveraging Moro’s electoral influence built during Operation Car Wash. His entry into the PL could impact the 2026 presidential election, as Paraná is seen as a pivotal state in tight national races.

item.title: A inusitada reação de Flavio Bolsonaro ao encontrar Lázaro Ramos em hotel
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) avoided a direct encounter with actor Lázaro Ramos by returning to his car upon seeing him arrive at a hotel in Rio de Janeiro's South Zone for the press conference of the film 'Velhos Bandidos'. The unusual scene, witnessed by bystanders in the lobby, drew comments from those present. Bolsonaro had just left a lunch with businessmen, while Ramos was preparing to promote the film set to premiere on March 26.

item.title: Os bastidores da filiação de Moro ao PL de Jair Bolsonaro
item.description: Former judge Sergio Moro is joining Bolsonaro's PL party following negotiations led by Senator Rogério Marinho, coordinator of Flávio's campaign. The move was discussed on February 23, focusing on Paraná's political landscape. The affiliation represents a strategic effort to strengthen Bolsonaro's base in the state.

item.title: Não vou permitir que feijão e carne subam por guerra, diz Lula
item.description: President Lula stated he will not allow the war between the U.S., Israel, and Iran to cause abusive increases in food and fuel prices, announcing measures to mitigate diesel price impacts, including urging states to reduce or eliminate ICMS on diesel, with federal compensation for half the revenue loss. He directed the Federal Police and Revenue Service to investigate unjustified price hikes and emphasized that the government will not accept automatic pass-through of international costs to consumers, especially truckers, to safeguard essential items like breakfast and school meals.

item.title: Bolsonaro: STM nega pedido para suspeição de ministro em julgamento sobre perda de posto e patente
item.description: The Superior Military Court (STM) rejected the defense's request to declare Vice-President Joseli Parente Camelo biased in the trial concerning Jair Bolsonaro's loss of rank and patent. The defense argued 'prejudgment' based on the minister's praise of Lula in 2023 interviews, but STM President Maria Elizabeth Rocha stated the remarks referred to hypothetical future military crimes, not the current case. The rapporteur is Minister Carlos Vuyk de Aquino, with review by Verônica Abdalla Sterman.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro afirma que manterá linha econômica do governo do pai dele se eleito
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro stated that if elected in 2026, he would maintain the same economic line as his father Jair Bolsonaro’s government, focusing on fiscal adjustment, spending less than revenue, and reducing taxes. He praised Paulo Guedes’ economic management, citing growth above China’s and inflation lower than the U.S.’s during the post-pandemic period, but avoided confirming whether Guedes would join his team. The senator also dismissed opinion polls as temporary snapshots and said voters are tired of four years of PT rule, calling Lula an 'expired product'.

item.title: Tarcísio se reúne com Moraes, Gilmar e Fachin em meio a ofensiva por domiciliar de Bolsonaro
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas is meeting with STF ministers this Thursday to discuss judicial actions of interest to the state, including the possibility of house arrest for former President Jair Bolsonaro. The meeting follows Bolsonaro’s hospitalization, which revived efforts by his defense to persuade Minister Alexandre de Moraes to reconsider the house arrest request, previously denied on the grounds that the Papuda prison can meet his medical needs. Although the official agenda includes the privatization of Sabesp, Bolsonaro’s health condition is expected to be a central topic of discussion.

item.title: Com Haddad candidato, Lula confirma Dario Durigan como novo ministro da Fazenda
item.description: President Lula confirmed that Dario Durigan, the executive secretary of the Ministry of Finance since 2023, will assume leadership of the portfolio in place of Fernando Haddad, who is set to formalize his pre-candidacy for governor of São Paulo. The announcement was made during the 17th Federative Caravan in São Paulo, where Lula emphasized the continuity of the economic agenda and praised Durigan as the future head of the economic team. Haddad departed the ministry on Thursday after announcing his exit to run in the 2026 elections.

item.title: Brasil envia ajuda a Cuba em meio a cerco dos EUA
item.description: Brazil has sent 20,000 tons of rice, along with other food and medicine, to assist Cuba amid a worsening crisis following the cutoff of Venezuelan oil supplies after the U.S. detained Nicolás Maduro. The Brazilian government denounced the situation as an 'ideological persecution' and stated the aid is being delivered through the World Food Programme. The shipment includes husked and milled rice, black beans, powdered milk, and medicines, with initial medical supplies arriving by air.

item.title: Lula confirma presença em jantar de arrecadação do PT
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva confirmed his attendance at a fundraising dinner for the Workers' Party (PT), reinforcing his support for the party amid preparations for the 2026 elections. The event aims to raise funds for state and federal campaigns, with emphasis on mobilizing the party’s base. Lula’s confirmation is seen as a signal of strengthening the PT’s political articulation for the upcoming election.

item.title: Influenciador conhecido como ‘O Brasileiro’ lança candidatura para eleições em Roma
item.description: Massimiliano Minnocci, an Italian influencer known as 'O Brasileiro' due to his support for AS Roma, announced an independent candidacy for Rome's Municipal Council in 2025, after initially targeting the mayoral race. He aims to represent the city's peripheries, citing his limited education (equivalent to sixth grade) and past careers as a singer, erotic content producer, and restaurant owner. Despite a conviction for threatening a police officer and a dropped accusation of assaulting an ex-partner, he claims to seek redemption by giving voice to those who have made mistakes in life.

item.title: Me preocupo com antecipar coisa sem necessidade, diz Flávio sobre divulgação de plano de governo
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for president, said he fears prematurely unveiling projects without necessity when releasing his government plan. He emphasized that he prefers to avoid premature exposure of proposals to prevent unrealistic expectations or unnecessary wear and tear. The statement was made on Thursday, March 19, 2026.

item.title: Em evento em São Paulo, Lula confirma Dario Durigan como substituto de Haddad
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva confirmed during an event in São Paulo that Dario Durigan, the executive secretary of the Ministry of Finance, will replace Fernando Haddad as head of the ministry, as Haddad prepares to run for governor of São Paulo. Lula urged the public to get to know Durigan, stating he would be held accountable for economic outcomes. Haddad, also present, reflected on his tenure, thanking Congress and emphasizing the restored federal pact as key to Brazil’s economic progress.

item.title: Ocaso da democracia dos EUA sob Trump é má notícia para o Brasil
item.description: The annual V-Dem report, a leading global monitor of democracy, confirms the accelerated deterioration of democracy in the United States under Donald Trump’s government, labeling it a significant democratic backslide. This trend is viewed as bad news for Brazil, as it weakens a key pillar of the liberal international order and may embolden similar authoritarian movements in other countries, including Brazil.

item.title: Estados devem recusar proposta do governo para reduzir ICMS nos combustíveis; Fazenda diz que está disposta a negociar
item.description: State governors are rejecting the federal government's proposal to zero the ICMS tax on diesel imports until May, citing concerns over state revenue and fiscal autonomy. Rio Grande do Sul has presented a counterproposal for discussion at the Confaz, while the Finance Ministry says it remains open to negotiation. President Lula reiterated the call for ICMS reduction on fuels, linking it to the effects of the Middle East war.

item.title: Lula solta palavrão para criticar redução da Selic pelo Banco Central
item.description: President Lula criticized the Central Bank's 0.25 percentage point cut in the Selic rate, saying he expected a 0.5% reduction and blaming the decision on the Iran war, which began on February 28 with coordinated attacks by the U.S. and Israel. He stated the government is making sacrifices to grow the economy, create jobs, and raise wages, while financial markets revised their expectations due to rising oil prices and geopolitical uncertainty.

item.title: Lula anuncia substituto de Haddad na Fazenda
item.description: President Lula announced that Dario Durigan, the executive secretary of the Ministry of Finance, will replace Fernando Haddad as the head of the ministry. The change will take effect after Haddad's official announcement, with Lula urging the public to hold Durigan accountable for economic policy.

item.title: Lula critica impactos da guerra do Irã, cobra ação da ONU e fala em abusos nos preços de combustíveis
item.description: President Lula criticized abusive fuel price increases, arguing they are not solely justified by the Iran conflict, and pledged government action to prevent the war from raising living costs, including investigations by the Federal Police, Revenue Service, and consumer protection agencies. He also urged the UN Security Council to act preventively, criticizing its five permanent members for failing to avoid wars and highlighting the global imbalance between military spending and widespread hunger.

item.title: Lula abre palanque para Haddad e diz que prefeitos de SP são mal recebidos por Tarcísio
item.description: Lula cited criticisms from mayors against Governor Tarcísio de Freitas on the same day he is expected to officially announce Fernando Haddad as the pre-candidate for the São Paulo government.

item.title: A surpresa de Valdemar ao tratar dos planos eleitorais de Hélio Lopes
item.description: PL national president Valdemar Costa Neto was surprised to learn that Deputy Hélio Lopes plans to change his electoral domicile to Roraima to run for Senate, following Jair Bolsonaro’s guidance. This move could disrupt the PL’s electoral alliances in the state ahead of the October elections. Valdemar attempted to dissuade him, noting that Lopes’ TCU candidacy might help PT candidate Odair Cunha win.

item.title: Lula diz estar 'triste' com decisão do BC de reduzir taxa de juros em 'só' 0,25 ponto percentual
item.description: President Lula expressed disappointment with the Copom's decision to cut the Selic rate by only 0.25 percentage point, from 15% to 14.75%, saying he had expected a 0.5-point reduction. He blamed the central bank's caution on the effects of the Iran war, criticizing external influences on domestic monetary policy.

item.title: Moro firma compromisso com PL e se filiará ao partido para disputar governo do Paraná
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro will leave União Brasil and join the PL to run for governor of Paraná in 2026, following a history of conflict with the party, including attempts to cassate his mandate in 2022 and 2023. His move marks a break with Governor Ratinho Junior (PSD), who is expected to run for president, and comes after meetings in Brasília with Valdemar Costa Neto and Flávio Bolsonaro, who praised Moro as the PL’s pre-candidate for Paraná governor.

item.title: Lula anuncia substituto de Haddad no Ministério da Fazenda
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad announced he will leave the Ministry of Finance this Thursday, with his removal to be published in the Official Gazette by Friday. His successor will be Dario Durigan, the current second-in-command, chosen by Lula as the natural replacement. Haddad is expected to launch his pre-candidacy for governor of São Paulo within the next 24 hours to challenge incumbent Tarcísio de Freitas.

item.title: Silvio Costa Filho desiste do Senado e vai concorrer à Câmara
item.description: Minister of Ports and Airports Silvio Costa Filho (Republicans) withdrew from the Senate race and announced he will run for re-election as a federal deputy in Pernambuco in 2026. The decision followed talks with President Lula, his party, and political allies, and was driven by poor polling numbers showing him at 10%-13% voter intent, behind Marília Arraes and Humberto Costa.

item.title: Haddad confirma saída da Fazenda e faz balanço da gestão exaltando pacto federativo
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad confirmed his departure from the ministry and presented a review of his economic management, emphasizing Congress’s role and the federal pact in driving recovery. He cited policies such as raising the income tax exemption threshold to R$ 5,000 and taxing high incomes, including previously exempt dividends, while praising cross-partisan support for states through initiatives like Propag. Haddad is also expected to run for governor of São Paulo in 2026 at Lula’s request.

item.title: Cláudio Castro decidiu renunciar ao governo do Rio para evitar cassaçăo, dizem aliados | Casado
item.description: Three allies of Rio de Janeiro Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) stated he decided to resign from office to avoid a potential removal by the TSE, whose trial is set to resume on Tuesday (24). The information was reported by UOL columnist Leticia Casado, based on sources close to the governor. The resignation would be a strategy to prevent losing his mandate due to electoral irregularities.

item.title: Lula reforça pedido a governadores por redução no ICMS sobre combustíveis
item.description: President Lula renewed his call for governors to reduce or exempt the ICMS tax on fuels to counter domestic impacts from the Middle East war. The federal government offers to compensate half of the states' revenue loss, with a decision expected by March 28 at an in-person meeting in São Paulo.

item.title: ‘Hoje é um dia especial, estou saindo do Ministério da Fazenda’, diz Haddad
item.description: Fernando Haddad announced today that he is leaving the Ministry of Finance to run for governor of São Paulo against Tarcísio de Freitas in 2026. He highlighted his proposals to exempt individuals earning up to R$5,000 from income tax and compensate the revenue loss by taxing the super-rich, while defending the Tax Reform as a measure against injustices faced by workers.

item.title: João Campos e Raquel Lyra dão largada para eleição em PE com times definidos e disputa por apoio de Lula
item.description: Recife Mayor João Campos (PSB) has defined his majoritarian ticket for the Pernambuco gubernatorial race in October, including Senator Humberto Costa (PT) seeking re-election and former deputy Marília Arraes (PDT) running for Senate. The official announcement is expected by week’s end. The alliance signals coordination with potential support from Lula.

item.title: Lula sanciona duas leis com R$ 3,6 bi para agricultura e indústria
item.description: President Lula signed two laws allocating R$ 3.6 billion to agriculture and industry: R$ 500 million for Pronaf via the Guarantee Fund and R$ 3.1 billion in tax incentives for Cubatão’s chemical industry, reducing PIS/Pasep and Cofins through 2027. The measures were announced at the 17th Federative Caravan in São Paulo, which also included investments in health, mobility, housing, education, and public safety.
